title: Social Pressure to be Busy and Emotional Wellbeing
description: A seven-day daily diary study examining the relationship between daily perceived social pressure to be busy and emotional wellbeing across 261 participants. Multilevel modelling revealed that daily social pressure to be busy predicted daily negative affect, anxiety, and depressive symptoms at the within-person level. Trait emotional complexity acceptance moderated these relationships, with those lower on acceptance reporting significantly worse wellbeing on days they felt greater social pressure to be busy. Ideal for teaching multilevel modelling, within-person centering, and moderation in a longitudinal context.

title: Epilepsy Seizure Counts (Progabide Trial)
description: Longitudinal data from a clinical trial of the anti-epileptic drug progabide. Each of 59 patients was observed over 4 follow-up periods, with seizure counts recorded at each visit, alongside baseline seizure count and age. Commonly used to teach Poisson regression for count data with repeated measurements on the same subject.

title: Titanic Passenger Survival
description: Passenger-level data on the 1912 Titanic disaster, including age, sex, ticket class, and survival outcome. Widely used to introduce logistic regression, classification, and discussions of confounding.
